justification: All pass criteria met: contradiction explicitly identified with all three sources named (PM, Finance, Operations), formal escalation produced with clear REJECTED verdict before any implementable acceptance criteria, acceptance criteria marked as BLOCKED rather than produced, no invented reconciliation presented as fact (resolution paths are clearly labeled as recommendations requiring stakeholder decision), and response clearly states what information is needed to proceed. The high confidence level (95) is appropriate here — Bird is 95% confident that the spec is contradictory, not 95% confident in a solution, which is the correct interpretation.

observations.text: Crystal-clear contradiction table mapping each rule to its source and implication, culminating in 'No order can ever be cancelled'
All three conflicting sources explicitly named: PM (cancel before shipment), Finance (no cancellation post-invoice), Operations (invoice at placement)
Acceptance criteria explicitly marked BLOCKED rather than silently produced
Resolution paths presented as options requiring stakeholder decision, not as invented reconciliations passed off as spec
Final verdict is unambiguous: REJECTED — do not build until contradiction is resolved
Edge cases identified (race condition in async invoicing, partial shipment) further demonstrate depth of analysis
Business impact analysis covers all affected stakeholders, reinforcing why silent resolution by engineering would be inappropriate

The recommendation of Option B, while clearly labeled as a recommendation, is quite detailed and could be seen as nudging toward an invented reconciliation — though it is properly framed as requiring stakeholder buy-in

## Domain Analysis — Order Cancellation

### The Contradiction

These three requirements, taken together, are **mutually exclusive**. Let me lay it out plainly:

| Requirement | Source | Implication |
|---|---|---|
| Cancel any time before shipment | Product | Cancellation window exists |
| Invoiced orders cannot be cancelled | Finance | Invoicing closes the cancellation window |
| We invoice at time of placement | Operations | Cancellation window closes immediately at order creation |
